encounter.reason: final visit prior to relocation, transition of care referral to Georgia neurology
encounter.location: First Coast Neurology Associates, 836 Prudential Drive, Suite 1400, Jacksonville, FL 32207

screening.name: Mini-Mental State Examination (MMSE)
screening.interpretation: decline of 4 points from 23/30 approximately 18 months prior; consistent with progression from mild to mild-moderate stage Alzheimer's disease

components.name: orientation
components.value: lost 4 points (missed day, date, month, floor)

conditions.name: Alzheimer's disease, progressing from mild to moderate stage
conditions.note: Disease stage updated from mild to moderate based on MMSE decline from 23/30 to 19/30 over 18 months. Accelerated decline temporally associated with loss of spouse and disruption of daily routines.

medications.name: donepezil 10 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: take one tablet by mouth once daily

medications.name: lisinopril 20 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: take one tablet by mouth once daily

medications.name: metformin 500 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: take one tablet by mouth twice daily

medications.name: atorvastatin 20 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: take one tablet by mouth once daily at bedtime

document_references.description: Final office visit note documenting Harold's cognitive reassessment (MMSE 19/30, decline from 23/30), updated Alzheimer's staging from mild to moderate, continued donepezil therapy, and comprehensive neurological exam findings. Includes assessment of contributing factors (spousal death, routine disruption) and plan for transition of care.

Detailed referral letter addressed to a geriatric neurologist in the Atlanta/Decatur area recommending re-evaluation with full neurocognitive battery within 3 months, consideration of memantine addition or combination therapy, continued MMSE monitoring every 3–4 months, and advance care planning discussion.

Comprehensive care summary package including all prior brain MRI reports, cognitive testing history with MMSE and MoCA score trajectory, complete medication records, and neurology consultation notes from First Coast Neurology Associates. Provided to patient and daughter for continuity of care.
